subject: Databases
title: Common Table Expressions
description: A 60-second lesson on Common Table Expressions.

hook.text: A complex query with three nested subqueries? CTEs break it into named, readable steps.

buildup.text: WITH active_users AS (SELECT ...) SELECT * FROM active_users. Name a subquery, use it like a table.

discovery.text: Chain multiple CTEs: WITH a AS (...), b AS (... FROM a) SELECT * FROM b. Each builds on the last.

twist.text: CTEs aren't always faster. Some databases treat them as optimization barriers. Check your query plan.

climax.text: Recursive CTEs walk trees: org charts, categories, file paths. WITH RECURSIVE is the key.

punchline.text: CTEs: named steps for complex queries. Clean and readable.

quiz.question: What is the main benefit of using CTEs over nested subqueries?
options: They break complex queries into named, readable steps
They always run faster than subqueries
They allow you to modify data in the database
